business.headline: China's economy slows to 4.3% in Q2 as domestic demand weakensOfficial data released by the National Bureau of Statistics (NBS) on Wednesday showed that the world's second-largest economy expanded by 4.3% between April and June, down from 5% in the first quarter.

Rs 62,500 crore push for mobile manufacturing: Will it make smartphones cheaper?The move is expected to help India strengthen its position as a global manufacturing hub and the scheme will run for five years, from FY 2026-27 to FY 2030-31.

India-UK FTA goes live: Why UK High Commissioner calls it the 'new gold standard'UK High Commissioner to India Lindy Cameron called the India-UK FTA the "new gold standard" for free trade agreements and said it should be viewed as the beginning of a deeper partnership rather than its final destination.

Sensex recovers but profit booking wipes out most gains amid oil concernsThe S&P BSE Sensex settled 130.49 points, or 0.17%, higher at 77,185.43 after climbing more than 500 points during the session. The NSE Nifty50 also erased a large part of its intraday gains to close 27.70 points, or 0.11%, higher at 24,078.50.

Centre approves Semicon 2.0 with Rs 1.27 lakh crore outlayThe Union Cabinet has approved Semicon 2.0 with a Rs 1.27 lakh crore outlay for chip design and manufacturing. The move seeks to expand India's role in the global semiconductor supply chain and reduce import dependence.

Files linked to Kudankulam N-power plant exposed in data breach: ReutersA ransomware group has published a large cache of stolen data on the dark web, claiming it includes blueprints of parts of India's largest nuclear power plant Kudankulam. The hackers said the data was obtained from Anil Ambani's Reliance Group, a contractor at the plant.

IBM lost over $100 billion in just 42 days. Should Indian IT investors worry?IBM's $100 billion market value erosion in 42 days has jolted technology stocks and raised questions for Indian IT. The sell-off reflects a wider shift towards AI infrastructure spending, though recent Indian IT earnings remain steadier.
Bihar to offer doorstep property registration for citizens aged 75 and aboveSenior citizens who wish to use the service must first book an appointment through Bihar's official e-registration portal.

A trade deal built for India's real economyFor most trade agreements, that gap between signature and implementation is a routine administrative window. For CETA, it has been the period in which the government has assembled the ground-level architecture that will determine whether the agreement delivers on its promise.

China's economy is slowing despite record exports. Here's what's going wrongChina's economy grew at its slowest pace in three years in the April-June quarter, even as exports surged. The gap underscores how a property slump, weak spending and job uncertainty are weighing on households.

Inside Fairfax's proposed $5.5 billion IDBI Bank acquisition and what's at stakeIf the proposed transaction goes through, it could be worth around $5.5 billion-$5.7 billion, making it one of the largest foreign investments ever in an Indian bank and one of the government's most significant privatisation deals in recent years.

Will your PF grow under India-UK social security pact? ExplainedWhile the India-UK trade pact has attracted attention for opening up markets and reducing tariffs, the social security agreement could have an equally meaningful impact for Indian professionals.

Top 10 changes as India-UK FTA kicks in: Cheaper cars, whisky, zero-duty exportsIndia's trade pact with the UK has come into effect, lowering duties and widening market access. The deal is expected to aid exporters, professionals and businesses while reshaping bilateral trade.

IBM's $70 billion wipeout: What triggered its worst stock crash in 58 years?IBM just suffered its worst stock crash in nearly six decades. What went wrong, and what does it reveal about the winners and losers of the AI race?
Dalal Street opens in green as banks lead gains, crude stays above $85The S&P BSE Sensex rose 530.01 points to 77,584.95, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 145.10 points to 24,197.15 as of 9:30 am.

Did AI help decide Meta's layoffs? Lawsuit makes a serious claimTwenty-six Meta employees have accused the company of using AI-assisted tools to score workers ahead of layoffs. The case could test how far employers can rely on such systems in workplace decisions.

From 500% to 100%: US eases tariff threat against India, China for Russian oil buysThe US Senate has updated a Russia sanctions bill by cutting the proposed tariff on buyers of Russian oil and gas. The change could ease pressure on major importers while giving Donald Trump room to waive penalties.

The changing face of India's gold crazeGold still holds a special place in Indian homes, but the reasons for buying it are quietly evolving. Is the yellow metal becoming less about tradition and more about investment?

IBM shares plunge 26% as AI spending shift hits software businessThe company has been trying to reduce its reliance on the cyclical mainframe business by focusing on its software unit including its high-margin Red Hat business, which helps firms run applications across different cloud providers.

India tightens import rules, bans goods produced using forced labourThe decision is expected to strengthen India's import regulations and encourage businesses to pay closer attention to their global supply chains.

entertainment.headline: The Batman 2: Robert Pattinson's first look revealed with new release date Actor Robert Pattinson's first look in The Batman 2 has been revealed with the actor in his batsuit. Director Matt Reeves also used the announcement to share the film's revised release date.

Actor Shehnaaz Gill cheers for 'good friend' Raghav Juyal, shuts down dating rumours Actor Shehnaaz Gill declined to address rumours linking her with Raghav Juyal and instead promoted his upcoming Bhai Tera Star Hai. The speculations about their relationship started

Anurag Kashyap stands by Sonam Wangchuk, calls the government's silence deafening Filmmaker Anurag Kashyap has backed Sonam Wangchuk as the activist continues his hunger strike for 18th day. His post sharpened focus on the government's silence as concerns grow over Wangchuk's health.

The Odyssey review: A timeless epic reborn through Christopher Nolan's geniusRelease Date:Fri Jul 17, 2026 Director Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey reimagines Odysseus' journey home as a vast modern epic. The film pairs mythic scale with the emotional toll of war, family separation and survival.

Actor Sivakarthikeyan meets CM Vijay ahead of Jana Nayagan release Actor Sivakarthikeyan met Tamil Nadu Chief Minister and actor Vijay ahead of Jana Nayagan's release and shared photographs from their meeting. In his note, he praised Vijay's journey and described their relationship as a continuing bond of brotherhood.

Vijay's Jana Nayagan overseas bookings open; Canada prioritises VIP members Advance ticket sales for Vijay's final film Jana Nayagan has started in several overseas markets, with Canada adopting a phased booking system that gives early access to VIP members.

Haiwaan: Akshay Kumar, Saif Ali Khan unleash vengeance in gritty first look The first look of Haiwaan hints at a sharp face-off between actors Akshay Kumar and Saif Ali Khan. The film also marks director Priyadarshan's official remake of his 2016 Malayalam film, Oppam.

No green screens, real ships, 91 days: How Nolan spent $250 million on The Odyssey The documentary The Odyssey: The Making of an Epic tracks Christopher Nolan's $250 million production across five countries without relying on green screens. It highlights a practical filmmaking approach shaped by real locations, physical training and historically rooted sound design.

Pawan Kalyan discharged after 4-day hospital stay following shoulder surgery Andhra Pradesh Deputy Chief Minister Pawan Kalyan has returned to Hyderabad after undergoing right shoulder surgery in Mumbai. Doctors have advised him to take three weeks of complete bed rest before beginning physiotherapy.

Jaaved Jaaferi says society is intolerant of comedy now, discusses Pranit More row Jaaved Jaaferi backed Pranit More's right to perform after the comedian faced backlash over a crowd-work clip. He said the reaction showed how comedians become easy targets and humour is now judged without context.

Rajinikanth reviews Vishnu Vishal's Gatta Kusthi 2, calls it a fun entertainer Rajinikanth reviewed Gatta Kusthi 2 as a "fun entertainer" and lauded Vishnu Vishal's script choices, Aishwarya Lekshmi's performance and child actor Zara's impressive screen presence.

Telugu film Chennai Love Story unfazed by Jana Nayagan, set for box office clash Actor Kiran Abbavaram reaffirmed that Chennai Love Story will release on July 24 despite a box-office clash with Vijay's final film, Jana Nayagan, expressing complete confidence in the film.

Director Basil Joseph's cryptic post sparks fresh Allu Arjun film speculation The filmmaker's latest social media post has reignited speculation about a possible film with Allu Arjun, though neither side has officially confirmed the project.

Ride or Die review: Hannah Waddingham, Octavia Spencer elevate this spy caperRelease Date:Wed Jul 15, 2026 Ride or Die follows Debbie as she learns her closest friend Judith is an assassin. The series, starring Hannah Waddingham and Octavia Spence, uses their long friendship to give its spy chaos an emotional centre.

The Odyssey is Indian at heart: How Nolan's epic fits perfectly into our tradition Director Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey is drawing Indian fans willing to pay Rs 3,100 for scarce IMAX seats. The film's deeper soul may lie in how the ancient Greek tale echoes Indian epics, karma and family honour.

Why Christopher Nolan fans are flying to Australia for The Odyssey Christopher Nolan fans are travelling thousands of miles to watch The Odyssey in Australia, turning a film screening into something closer to a pilgrimage. The film releases worldwide on July 18 and on July 17 in India.

Govt may mandate OTT certification, act against Zee5 amid Satluj row: Sources The Indian government is considering making CBFC certification mandatory for films released on OTT platforms following the uncertified streaming of Satluj on Zee5.

Ban censor board: RGV says censorship is killing Indian cinema in explosive rant Ram Gopal Varma has called for the censor board to be abolished, saying adults should choose what they watch. He argued that in the digital era, censorship is outdated and the industry should challenge it in court and public discourse.

Vijay's Jana Nayagan, 'built for history', finally gets worldwide release date Vijay's much-anticipated film Jana Nayagan is all set for a grand release worldwide after overcoming a prolonged legal battle and certification hurdles. Fans eagerly await this historic film, which is expected to be Vijay's final cinematic venture.

general news.headline: When Dharmendra Pradhan met Sonam WangchukWhen Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan and Ladakh-based activist and educationist Sonam Wangchuk held a meeting in 2023 over the National Education Policy, it reflected warmth and optimism. Three years later, Wangchuk is on an indefinite hunger strike, demanding Pradhan's resignation.

I spent a night as a cockroach at CJP's Jantar Mantar protest. This is what I sawI stayed overnight at the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protest site at New Delhi's Jantar Mantar without revealing my identity. I spoke to the participants, ate the food being served and overheard the conversations around fasting activist Sonam Wangchuk. I also noticed the unease over absent CJP leaders.

Crypto is haram: Top Pakistani cleric issues fatwa amid Trump's digital asset pushThe fatwa comes at a challenging time for Islamabad, which has been moving aggressively to develop a regulatory framework for the cryptocurrency sector. More than 20 million Pakistanis are estimated to be involved in cryptocurrency-related activities, with transactions worth billions of dollars reported in recent years.

ENG vs ARG Live: Line-ups out, Messi faces England for first timeEngland (ENG) vs Argentina (ARG) Live, FIFA World Cup 2026 semi-final: Nearly four decades after the Hand of God changed football history, England and Argentina meet again with a place in the FIFA World Cup final at stake. This time, Lionel Messi and Jude Bellingham carry the hopes of two nations.

The Odyssey review: A timeless epic reborn through Christopher Nolan's geniusRelease Date:Fri Jul 17, 2026Director Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey reimagines Odysseus' journey home as a vast modern epic. The film pairs mythic scale with the emotional toll of war, family separation and survival.

Are you Muslim? Indian man survives after being stabbed 15 times at US mallAn Indian Muslim man was stabbed inside a Utah shopping mall after being questioned about his religion. Police said the suspect admitted targeting him because of his religion and warned that he posed a serious threat to the public.

Sonam Wangchuk extremely critical, Centre responsible if anything happens: TMCTrinamool urged Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan to open talks with fasting activist Sonam Wangchuk, claiming his health had become critical after 18 days of hunger strike.

POK on edge as showdown looms: 12 killed, 4,000 Rangers deployed, media blacked outCities and towns in Pakistan-Occupied Kashmir (POK) have been sealed, and an undeclared media blackout has been imposed on Rawalakot ahead of the march to Muzaffarabad on Wednesday. At least 12 people were killed in Tuesday's clashes as the Pakistani establishment stepped up its crackdown.

Dutch woman thought Mumbai was her dream city, until Bengaluru changed her mindA Dutch woman shared why moving to Mumbai from Bengaluru no longer matched the life she needed after becoming a mother. Her video sparked conversation on how cities can feel different as priorities shift with life stages.

As Dharavi is set to disappear, a digital twin will ensure it survivesDharavi is being preserved in a detailed digital twin before redevelopment starts in full swing and reshapes the settlement, which is India's largest slum in Mumbai. The 3D archive is expected to guide relocation and planning while preserving a record of the neighbourhood that future generations will never experience.

He's fighting like a tiger: Mamata Banerjee backs Abhishek amid growing TMC revoltAddressing party workers on Facebook Live, Mamata Banerjee accused the BJP of engineering defections, apologised "on behalf of the traitors", and strongly backed Abhishek Banerjee for resisting intimidation.

Sharad Pawar's NCP may back NDA's delimitation bill, breaking ranks with OppositionThe Sharad Pawar-led NCP is likely to back the NDA's Delimitation Bill if it guarantees a uniform 50% increase in Lok Sabha seats across all states, a move that could see the party break ranks with the Opposition.

Sonali Bendre credits autophagy after cancer: What fasting for 18-20 hours really doesSonali Bendre's 18–20-hour fasting routine has sparked a debate around intermittent fasting and autophagy after the actor opened up about her post-cancer lifestyle. Doctors say fasting may support healthy habits, but it should not be mistaken for a cancer treatment or a universal health solution.

I am ready: Smriti Mandhana prepared to replace Harmanpreet as India captainSmriti Mandhana says she is ready to captain India in Harmanpreet Kaur's absence, with the vice-captain boasting an impressive record of 15 wins in 22 matches across T20Is and ODIs.

UK Hindu temple fights for survival after complex sold to Islamic group for mosqueFounded by Indian families fleeing to the UK from Uganda, the city of Peterborough's only Hindu temple is battling to save its home after the Labour-led city council sold the complex housing it to an Islamic group planning to build a mosque. The dispute is now before the UK High Court.

Live: IRGC threatens to shut more energy export routes after US's fresh strikesUS-Iran War Live Updates: US-Iran tensions escalated further on Wednesday as American forces launched another round of airstrikes on military targets inside Iran, saying the strikes were aimed at eliminating "emerging threats" and degrading capabilities used to attack commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. The fresh operation came ahead of a renewed US naval blockade on Iranian ports and coastal areas. Tehran responded by claiming it had struck military targets in Bahrain and Kuwait, including weapons depots and a drone launch ramp at Kuwait's Ali Al Salem Air Base, calling the attacks retaliation for recent US strikes. Kuwait later said it intercepted one ballistic missile, five cruise missiles and 33 drones, accusing Iran of targeting vital civilian and military facilities. One Kuwaiti Navy vessel was hit and four service members were injured. Iran also hardened its stance on the Strait of Hormuz, with the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps warning that "not a drop of oil and gas will be exported" from the region as long as US forces remain there, while a deputy foreign minister declared, "We will exercise our sovereignty on the Strait of Hormuz whatever that costs." Amid the military escalation, US President Donald Trump reversed his proposal to charge a 20 per cent fee on cargo transiting the strategic waterway just a day after announcing it. Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates.

From IIT to millionaire: Meet Siddhartha Saxena who earned Rs 77 crore in a single dayFrom IIT Kanpur to building a fast-growing AI startup, Siddhartha Saxena's journey is defined by innovation and calculated risk. The Merlin co-founder says he earned nearly Rs 77 crore (USD 8 million) in a single day, becoming a millionaire at just 26.

Madan Mitra's exit the final nail in coffin for Mamata?In a shock move, TMC MLA Madan Mitra quit Mamata Banerjee's camp and joined the rebel Trinamool faction, blaming Abhishek Banerjee for the crisis. The move carries symbolic weight because Mitra had stood by Mamata through the party's rise and setbacks.

Monsoon warming up in Bay of Bengal after power nap. When will it rain?Something brewing in the Bay of Bengal is expected to revive the monsoon in coming days, bringing widespread rain to several states. Read when rain will arrive and where.

The rank 1 team never wins. France is the latest to fallSpain beat France 2-0 in Dallas to knock the world's top-ranked side out of the World Cup semi-final. The defeat extended France's run of exits to Spain and preserved the tournament's long-standing No. 1 ranking hoodoo.

Kia Syros EV unveiled with 526km range, bookings open at Rs 25,000Kia's second mass-market electric SUV promises a segment-leading 526km certified range, 39-minute fast charging, lifetime battery warranty and Level 2 ADAS as the automaker looks to accelerate EV adoption in India.

NEET aspirant dies by suicide after anxiety over exam performance in RajasthanFollowing the incident, police reached the hospital, took custody of the body and sent it for post-mortem. The body was handed over to the family later in the evening after the post-mortem examination.

Govt may mandate OTT certification, act against Zee5 amid Satluj row: SourcesThe Indian government is considering making CBFC certification mandatory for films released on OTT platforms following the uncertified streaming of Satluj on Zee5.

IPL-style eliminator, Super 7: ICC announces major changes to ODI, T20 World CupsThe ICC's major tournament revamp introduces a unified Super 7 round-robin stage for the men's ODI World Cup and brings high-stakes, IPL-style crossover eliminators to an expanded Super 10 phase in the T20 World Cup. We have tried to explain the new formats. Read on.

sports.headline: ENG vs ARG Live: Line-ups out, Messi faces England for first time England (ENG) vs Argentina (ARG) Live, FIFA World Cup 2026 semi-final: Nearly four decades after the Hand of God changed football history, England and Argentina meet again with a place in the FIFA World Cup final at stake. This time, Lionel Messi and Jude Bellingham carry the hopes of two nations.

Ben Stokes returns to Durham with 'new lease of life' after England retirement Ben Stokes returns to Durham for the first time since retiring from international cricket, beginning a new chapter far from England's spotlight. The One-Day Cup opener offers the all-rounder a chance to rediscover the joy of playing where it all began.

Axar Patel makes big jump in ICC ODI rankings after Birmingham heroics Axar Patel has climbed three places to ninth in the ICC ODI all-rounders' rankings after his match-winning display of 4/62 and an unbeaten 57 in India's six-wicket victory over England in Birmingham.

BCCI secretary Devajit Saikia to head ICC's Governance Review Committee BCCI secretary Devajit Saikia has been appointed head of the ICC's newly formed Governance Review Committee and has also been included in the Franchise Leagues Committee led by former Bangladesh captain Tamim Iqbal.

Carlos Alcaraz completely healed from injury, US Open return hopes brighten Carlos Alcaraz has made a full recovery from the wrist injury that sidelined him for nearly four months, with reports claiming that his medical team is satisfied with his progress ahead of a possible US Open return.

IPL-style eliminator, Super 7: ICC announces major changes to ODI, T20 World Cups The ICC's major tournament revamp introduces a unified Super 7 round-robin stage for the men's ODI World Cup and brings high-stakes, IPL-style crossover eliminators to an expanded Super 10 phase in the T20 World Cup. We have tried to explain the new formats. Read on.

How to watch England vs Argentina World Cup semis: Date, time, streaming details England and Argentina will renew one of football's fiercest rivalries in the FIFA World Cup 2026 semi-final in Atlanta, with the winners advancing to face Spain in Sunday's final.

Rohit Sharma gets coach Gautam Gambhir's backing amid ODI World Cup speculation India head coach Gautam Gambhir has backed Rohit Sharma to continue playing his natural game, with the team management keen to free the veteran opener from any pressure over his future.

I am ready: Smriti Mandhana prepared to replace Harmanpreet as India captain Smriti Mandhana says she is ready to captain India in Harmanpreet Kaur's absence, with the vice-captain boasting an impressive record of 15 wins in 22 matches across T20Is and ODIs.

India falters in Tokyo: Lakshya and Ayush knocked out in first round Lakshya Sen and Ayush Shetty joined India's early exits at the Japan Open after first-round defeats on Wednesday. PV Sindhu is the lone Indian shuttler to reach the second round of singles in Tokyo.

Selfless template gone, India back to old-fashioned grind ahead of 2027 World Cup India's commanding win over England in the first ODI was more than just a morale-boosting result after the T20I debacle. It also offered the clearest indication yet that Shubman Gill's India are embracing a more patient, calculated ODI template with the 2027 World Cup in South Africa firmly in mind.

Freeze Kane: Argentina fans pin hopes on bizarre superstition before England clashArgentina fans are sticking to their cherished cabalas ahead of the World Cup semi-final against England, believing rituals such as freezing Harry Kane's name and repeating matchday routines can help the defending champions reach another final.

Teenage swagger: At 19, Lamine Yamal walks the talk, escapes the trolls in styleLamine Yamal's pre-match swagger was vindicated as Spain beat France 2-0 in the World Cup semi-final. His sharp display and Spain's control left the pre-tournament favourites chasing shadows.

IND vs ENG: Sundar credits coach Gambhir for unlocking his batting potentialWashington Sundar credited India head coach Gautam Gambhir and Gujarat Titans coach Ashish Nehra for helping him evolve as a batter after his unbeaten half-century guided India to a six-wicket victory over England in the first ODI at Edgbaston on Tuesday.

Atlanta Police tighten security for England vs Argentina World Cup semi-finalThe Atlanta Police Department (APD), in a statement on Tuesday, said it had enhanced security measures for Wednesday's FIFA World Cup 2026 semi-final between England and Argentina at Atlanta Stadium, deploying additional personnel and resources to ensure the match passes off safely.

Stop Messi at all costs? England coach Tuchel considers masterplan for semi-finalEngland coach Thomas Tuchel said he has considered using an old-school man-marking plan to contain Lionel Messi when his side face Argentina in Wednesday's FIFA World Cup semi-final.

FIFA chief Infantino under IOC scrutiny over Balogun World Cup ban reversalThe International Olympic Committee (IOC) has received a complaint seeking an investigation into FIFA president Gianni Infantino's alleged role in reversing Folarin Balogun's World Cup suspension. It also accuses Infantino of repeatedly breaching political neutrality rules through his support for US President Donald Trump.

Argentina vs England: Coach Scaloni outlines formula to reach World Cup finalArgentina coach Lionel Scaloni has urged his players to combine their trademark grit with their attacking quality when they take on England in the FIFA World Cup semi-final on Wednesday, insisting that determination alone will not be enough to secure a place in the final.

England vs Argentina semifinal guide: Messi steps into football's fiercest rivalryEngland vs Argentina semi-final: England and Argentina do not meet as ordinary opponents. Rooted in Maradona's infamous 1986 quarter-final, this rivalry carries weight few international fixtures can match. Now Messi faces the one team that has always eluded him with a spot in the final against Spain up for grabs.

Lionel Messi's perfect-record referee to officiate Argentina vs England semi-finalFIFA has appointed Ismail Elfath to officiate Argentina's World Cup semi-final against England in Atlanta. Messi has been unbeaten in all five matches involving the Moroccan-American referee.

How Pedri and Lamine Yamal manifested Spain's World Cup final into existencePedri and Lamine Yamal's old Instagram post has resurfaced after Spain reached the World Cup final on 19 July. What once seemed cheeky now reflects the belief driving Spain's young side into a record-chasing title match.

Spain have found their 2010 World Cup spirit: De La Fuente after France winSpain beat France 2-0 in Dallas to reach the 2026 World Cup final, after which Luis de la Fuente said his side had rediscovered the spirit of 2010. The coach said the squad's unity, humility and collective mindset have put La Roja one win from a second world title.

Kylian Mbappe owns up to France's poor display vs Spain in World Cup semifinalKylian Mbappe admitted France were second best after a 2-0 World Cup semi-final defeat to Spain, saying Les Bleus fell short tactically and technically. France will now face the third-place playoff, Didier Deschamps' final match before his expected departure.

Mbappe, Dembele, Olise didn't turn up for France: Patrick Vieira on Spain defeatPatrick Vieira tore into France after their 2-0 World Cup semi-final defeat to Spain. He said the team's biggest players failed collectively as Spain's discipline exposed them.

Didier Deschamps questions referee, but France had bigger problems against SpainFrance vs Spain, FIFA World Cup semifinal: France coach Didier Deschamps questioned the referee after the World Cup semi-final loss to Spain but admitted Les Bleus were second best as Rodri and Spain's midfield dismantled their attacking threat.

Spain strip Mbappe-led France of their aura to reach World Cup finalFIFA World Cup 2026: Kylian Mbappe, Ousmane Dembele and Michael Olise had lit up the World Cup. Spain switched off the lights, producing their most complete display of the tournament to march into only the second World Cup final in their history.

FIFA fever goes traditional: World Cup themed dhotis become hot favouritesKerala's football fever has found a unique local expression during the FIFA World Cup, with football-themed dhotis flying off the shelves alongside team jerseys. Shopkeepers say Argentina, Brazil and Portugal-inspired mundus have become some of the most sought-after items this tournament.

Bumrah's sorcery, Axar's resilience hand India first win on UK tourIndia won their first match of the tour of United Kingdom on Tuesday, 14 July. Jasprit Bumrah, returning to the ODI team for the first time since November 2023, once again set the tone with his opening spell of the match.

Lord's century is a dream come true: Yastika Bhatia relishing historic 100Yastika Bhatia called her historic unbeaten 113 at Lord's a "dream come true" after becoming the first woman to score a Test century at the iconic venue. The landmark knock capped an inspiring comeback from the ACL injury that sidelined her in late 2025.

FRA 0-2 ESP: Spain outplay France to make it to World Cup finalFrance (FRA) vs Spain (ESP) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026 semi-final: (FRA 0-2 ESP) Spain make another double substitution as Marcos Llorente replaces Pedro Porro and Nico Williams comes on for Alex Baena. With an 82nd-minute 2-0 lead, La Roja are freshening up their side as they close in on the World Cup final.

Tom Cruise to IShowSpeed headline FIFA World Cup 2026 closing ceremonyFIFA has confirmed Tom Cruise, IShowSpeed and a string of music stars for the World Cup 2026 closing ceremony in New York New Jersey. The pre-match show is set to cap the first 48-team tournament before the final decides the champions.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
